title: China-to-Burkina Faso Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 for PV Inverters — ABNORM and Donor / Lender Procurement
rows.target_requirement: ABNORM (Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité) adopts IEC standards as the national reference for Burkina Faso. For PV inverter EMC, the applicable IEC standards are: IEC 62920 (Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment) and IEC 61000-3-2 (Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions for equipment with input current ≤16 A per phase). Burkina Faso does not have a separately published national mandatory EMC regulation specifically for PV inverters as of the knowledge cutoff; however, IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 EMC test evidence is expected by: (a) SONABEL and ARSE as part of grid-connection and licensing technical review for grid-tied inverters; (b) donor/lender procurement specifications (World Bank, AFD, AfDB, EU) for both grid-tied and off-grid project equipment. Burkina Faso is a francophone country; French-language technical documentation alongside English IEC test reports is recommended for ABNORM, ARSE, and SONABEL submission.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase)
ABNORM — Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité (national standards and conformity body — IEC adoption authority)
ARSE — Autorité de Régulation du Secteur de l'Énergie (energy regulator — licensing technical review includes EMC evidence)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC is assessed under GB/T 37408-2019 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which includes EMC test requirements aligned with China's domestic framework. GB/T 37408 EMC testing is conducted at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. While IEC 62920 draws on IEC 61000-series test methods that overlap with Chinese domestic standards, the GB/T 37408 EMC test scope and referenced limits are not harmonised with IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 as applied by ABNORM, SONABEL, and ARSE. Chinese EMC certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 are not accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 compliance evidence for Burkina Faso project submissions or donor/lender procurement.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC test requirements
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC testing does not satisfy ABNORM, SONABEL, ARSE, or donor/lender IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements for Burkina Faso. Exporters should obtain: (a) IEC 62920 EMC type-test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model; (b) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission test report for the model. These reports should be specific to the power rating and configuration of the inverter being supplied. Note that Burkina Faso's grid voltage (220/380 V, 50 Hz) is nominally the same as China's grid parameters, so EMC test results obtained under Chinese grid conditions may be applicable without retesting — however, this should be confirmed with the project EMC engineer and SONABEL, as test conditions and installation environment (Sahel heat, dust) may affect harmonic behaviour. Additionally, French-language summaries of IEC EMC test results are recommended for regulatory submissions to ARSE and SONABEL in Burkina Faso.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for donor/lender-financed projects and SONABEL/ARSE grid-connection technical review; no confirmed standalone mandatory national EMC regulation specifically for PV inverters in Burkina Faso as of 2026-06-14 — verify with ABNORM before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 EMC testing is a requirement for voluntary CQC/CGC certification; not a standalone mandatory market-access requirement for domestic China PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates do not satisfy ABNORM, SONABEL, ARSE, or donor/lender IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements in Burkina Faso. Obtain IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 type-test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory for the specific inverter model. As Burkina Faso's grid voltage (220/380 V, 50 Hz) is nominally identical to China's, EMC test results at Chinese grid parameters may be reusable — confirm with the project engineer and SONABEL before assuming equivalence. Prepare French-language technical summaries for regulatory submissions to ARSE and SONABEL.

source.publisher: ABNORM — Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité (Burkina Faso)

rows.requirement: SONABEL Grid-Connection Standards — Anti-Islanding (IEC 62116), IEC 61727, and 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: SONABEL (Société Nationale d'Électricité du Burkina) governs grid connection of solar PV inverters on Burkina Faso's national electricity network. Burkina Faso's grid operates at 50 Hz, single-phase 220 V, three-phase 380 V — consistent with West African ECOWAS/CEDEAO regional grid standards and with IEC 60038 standard voltages. ARSE (Autorité de Régulation du Secteur de l'Énergie) is the sector regulator whose authorisation is required before grid-tied generation equipment may be connected. ABNORM (Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité) adopts IEC standards as the national reference. The applicable IEC grid-connection standards for PV inverters include: (1) IEC 62116 anti-islanding test — inverters must detect loss of mains and disconnect within 2 seconds maximum; (2) IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface, setting voltage and frequency disconnection thresholds; (3) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission limits. Inverter firmware and protection relay settings must be validated against Burkina Faso's 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid parameters. Although Burkina Faso's grid voltage is nominally the same as China's (220/380 V at 50 Hz under GB/T 156), SONABEL's own project specifications and donor/lender procurement rules reference IEC-based test evidence rather than Chinese GB/T documentation.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions
IEC 60038 — IEC standard voltages (reference for 220/380 V, 50 Hz West African grid)
ARSE — Autorité de Régulation du Secteur de l'Énergie (Burkina Faso energy sector regulator — licensing authority for grid-tied generation)
SONABEL — Société Nationale d'Électricité du Burkina (national utility, grid-connection technical authority)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System, applicable to utility-scale PV via 10 kV and above), NB/T 32004 (inverter technical specifications), and domestic anti-islanding test results. While Burkina Faso and China nominally share the same grid voltage (220/380 V at 50 Hz), SONABEL project specifications and donor/lender procurement rules (World Bank, AFD, AfDB) require IEC-referenced type-test evidence rather than Chinese GB/T documentation. Chinese grid-connection documents do not satisfy SONABEL's IEC-based technical review or ARSE licensing requirements for imported PV equipment.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Although grid voltage parameters are nominally identical (220/380 V, 50 Hz), Chinese GB/T grid-connection documents are not accepted by SONABEL or ARSE as evidence for grid-tied PV inverter approval in Burkina Faso. Exporters must prepare: (a) IEC 62116 anti-islanding test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory; (b) IEC 61727 utility-interface compliance evidence; (c) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic emission compliance evidence; (d) ARSE authorisation as part of project permitting — confirm current licensing pathway and documentation requirements with ARSE directly; (e) SONABEL grid-connection application with IEC-compliant technical documentation. Where the project is donor/lender financed (World Bank, AFD, AfDB, EU), the procurement specification will typically mandate IEC type-test evidence independently of local regulatory requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection under SONABEL technical requirements and ARSE licensing; donor/lender-financed projects impose IEC type-test requirements as a procurement condition independent of local regulatory requirements
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China's grid operator requirements but is not recognised as equivalent by SONABEL, ARSE, or donor/lender procurement specifications in Burkina Faso

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Despite sharing nominal grid voltage (220/380 V, 50 Hz) with China, Burkina Faso's SONABEL and ARSE require IEC-based technical evidence for grid-tied PV inverters — Chinese GB/T grid certificates are not accepted as equivalent. Obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding, IEC 61727 utility-interface, and IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic emission test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory before submitting to SONABEL and ARSE. For donor/lender-financed projects, confirm procurement standard requirements with the project owner before finalising the equipment compliance package.

source.publisher: ARSE — Autorité de Régulation du Secteur de l'Énergie (Burkina Faso)

rows.requirement: Off-Grid and Mini-Grid PV Inverters — ABER / FDE and IEC TS 62257 Series
rows.target_requirement: A significant portion of Burkina Faso's solar PV deployment is in off-grid and mini-grid systems serving rural communities with low electrification rates. The Agence Burkinabè de l'Electrification Rurale (ABER, formerly FDE — Fonds de Développement de l'Énergie) manages rural electrification programmes and applies IEC TS 62257 series specifications for stand-alone and mini-grid PV systems. Key applicable standards include: IEC TS 62257-1 (recommendations for rural electrification), IEC TS 62257-7-1 (PV array specifications), IEC TS 62257-8-1 (selection of electrical appliances), and IEC TS 62257-9-5 (integrated photovoltaic systems — particular specifications for SHS). Donor-financed mini-grid and SHS projects (World Bank, EU WACA, AFD, AfDB) routinely require IEC TS 62257-series compliance as a procurement condition. ARSE licensing applies to mini-grid operators. ABNORM serves as the national standards reference body adopting IEC standards.
target_standards: IEC TS 62257-1 — Recommendations for small renewable energy and hybrid systems for rural electrification — Part 1: General introduction
IEC TS 62257-7-1 — Recommendations for small renewable energy and hybrid systems for rural electrification — Part 7-1: Generators — Photovoltaic arrays

IEC TS 62257-9-5 — Recommendations for small renewable energy and hybrid systems for rural electrification — Part 9-5: Integrated system — Selection and design, installation and maintenance of rural electrification systems — Particular specifications for SHS
ABER — Agence Burkinabè de l'Electrification Rurale (rural electrification authority — off-grid and mini-grid programme specifications)
ARSE — Autorité de Régulation du Secteur de l'Énergie (licensing authority for mini-grid operators)
rows.china_equivalent: China does not have a directly equivalent rural electrification standards framework referencing IEC TS 62257. Chinese off-grid PV system standards include GB/T 19064 (Solar home system — Technical requirements and test methods) and relevant NB/T standards for stand-alone PV systems. These Chinese off-grid standards are based on China's domestic conditions and are not recognised by ABER, ARSE, or donor/lender procurement specifications in Burkina Faso as equivalent to IEC TS 62257 series evidence.
china_standards: GB/T 19064-2003 — 家用太阳能光伏电源系统技术条件和试验方法 (Solar home system — Technical requirements and test methods)
NB/T 32012-2013 — 光伏发电站太阳能资源实时监测技术要求 (relevant NB/T off-grid PV standards)
rows.gap: Gap: For off-grid and mini-grid PV inverters in Burkina Faso, Chinese domestic off-grid standards (GB/T 19064, relevant NB/T standards) are not accepted by ABER, ARSE, or donor/lender procurement programmes as equivalent to IEC TS 62257 series compliance. Exporters must: (a) confirm applicable IEC TS 62257 part(s) with the project owner or ABER/FDE programme manager; (b) provide IEC TS 62257-compliant technical documentation and test evidence; (c) confirm ARSE licensing requirements for the mini-grid operator; (d) verify project-specific donor/lender procurement standards before equipment selection. Note that Chinese inverter manufacturers supplying Sahel markets commonly adapt products for high-temperature operation; however, adaptation alone does not substitute for IEC TS 62257 type-test documentation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for donor/lender-financed off-grid and mini-grid projects where IEC TS 62257 is specified; ARSE licensing mandatory for mini-grid operators; ABER programme specifications apply to rural electrification tender lots
mandatory_status.cn: No direct Chinese mandatory equivalent for IEC TS 62257 off-grid/mini-grid specifications; GB/T 19064 is a voluntary recommended standard for solar home systems under Chinese law

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Off-grid and mini-grid PV inverters for Burkina Faso projects must meet IEC TS 62257 series requirements as specified by ABER programme managers and donor/lender procurement rules — Chinese domestic off-grid standards are not accepted as equivalent. Confirm the applicable IEC TS 62257 parts and donor/lender procurement requirements with the project owner before equipment selection. ARSE licensing requirements for mini-grid operators must be verified directly with ARSE before project commissioning.

source.publisher: ABER — Agence Burkinabè de l'Electrification Rurale (Burkina Faso)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — ABNORM Conformity and Donor / Lender Procurement
rows.target_requirement: ABNORM (Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité) is Burkina Faso's national standards body and adopts IEC standards as the primary national reference for electrical and electronic equipment. ABNORM is a member of ARSO (African Organisation for Standardisation) and participates in ECOWAS/CEDEAO regional harmonisation. For solar PV inverters, the applicable safety standards are IEC 62109-1 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters). Third-party type-test evidence from an ILAC-accredited laboratory is expected for: (a) ABNORM conformity assessment where a conformity scheme applies to imported electrical equipment; (b) donor/lender procurement specifications (World Bank, AFD, AfDB, EU) which routinely require IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates as a condition of equipment supply. Burkina Faso does not have a published national mandatory conformity scheme specifically for PV inverters as of the knowledge cutoff; however, ABNORM conformity marking requirements for imported electrical equipment should be verified with ABNORM directly before shipment. Additionally, the Sahel climate imposes elevated ambient temperature conditions (routinely ≥45 °C) and high dust levels; IEC 62109-1 environmental protection ratings (IP54 or better recommended for dusty outdoor Sahel environments) and rated output derating at elevated temperatures must be documented in the equipment technical file.
target_standards: IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements (parent standard, implicitly applicable)
ABNORM — Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité (national standards body — conformity assessment authority for Burkina Faso)
IEC 60529 — Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code) — applicable to environmental protection rating for Sahel dusty conditions
rows.china_equivalent: China's primary voluntary standard for PV grid-connected inverter safety is GB/T 37408-2019 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which covers classification, environmental conditions, safety, electrical performance, EMC, and markings. GB/T 37408 is assessed by CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. It shares conceptual safety intent with IEC 62109-1/-2 but is not harmonised with IEC 62109 and is not accepted by ABNORM, donor/lender procurement programmes, or SONABEL project engineers as equivalent to IEC 62109 type-test evidence. Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 do not satisfy the IEC 62109-1/-2 requirements expected in Burkina Faso project procurement.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 safety certification does not satisfy ABNORM's IEC-referenced conformity assessment or donor/lender IEC 62109-1/-2 procurement requirements for Burkina Faso. Exporters must: (a) obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model or family; (b) verify current ABNORM conformity marking requirements for imported electrical equipment before shipment; (c) confirm that the inverter datasheet demonstrates rated output at ≥45 °C ambient temperature (Sahel derating) and appropriate IP protection rating (IP54 or better recommended for dusty outdoor installation); (d) confirm IEC 62109-1/-2 test scope covers the environmental stress class applicable to the Sahel climate zone. Existing Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 should be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test reports before submitting equipment documentation for ABNORM conformity assessment or donor/lender procurement.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for donor/lender-financed project procurement; ABNORM conformity assessment requirements for imported electrical equipment should be verified directly with ABNORM — no confirmed published mandatory product-specific scheme for PV inverters identified as of 2026-06-14
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended national standard (GB/T designation); CQC/CGC voluntary certification available but not required under Chinese law

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A solar PV inverter holding only Chinese GB/T 37408 certification cannot satisfy ABNORM IEC-based conformity assessment or donor/lender IEC 62109-1/-2 procurement requirements in Burkina Faso. Plan for IEC 62109-1/-2 type testing by an ILAC-accredited laboratory, with test conditions covering elevated ambient temperature (≥45 °C, Sahel) and appropriate IP protection for dusty outdoor environments. Verify current ABNORM conformity marking and import requirements directly with ABNORM before shipment.

source.publisher: ABNORM — Agence Burkinabè de Normalisation, de la Métrologie et de la Qualité (Burkina Faso)
